since_last: 32% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +2.31%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 32% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +0.10% · BTC -2.65% · Gold +1.52% · Nasdaq100 +0.86%. 32% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +2.31%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +2.31%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -2.90%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +17.20%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +17.20%, AMAT +10.56%, TER +8.68%, KLAC +7.39%, QCOM +5.50%. Laggards: INOD -11.24%, AI -6.01%, DELL -5.84%, PLTR -4.86%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -0.73% at $153.41
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -0.73% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
